get.description:
Get usage summary for a clients (sessions, etc.)

Args:
    current_user: Authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Standardized response with usage summary data

Raises:
    HTTPException: If the user doesn't have access to this accounts

post.description: Discover tools from a custom MCP server

get.description:
Get execution metrics with optional filters.
- Regular users can only see metrics for their own client.
- Admins can see all metrics, or filter by a specific account_id.

post.description:
Create a new session for a specific agent and user.
Returns a unique session_id that can be used for subsequent chat interactions.
Optionally accepts metadata (name, description, tags) for the session.

Supports both authenticated users (via bearer token) and agent bots (via API key).
For agent bots, session_id and user_id can be provided in the request body.

delete.description:
Bulk delete multiple sessions.

This endpoint allows deleting multiple sessions at once. It validates that the user
has access to each session before deletion. Returns a summary of the operation
including successful deletions and any failures.

get.description:
Gets messages from a session with embedded artifacts.

This function loads all messages from a session and processes any references
to artifacts, loading them and converting them to base64 for direct use in the frontend.
Get metadata for a specific session

put.description: Update metadata for a specific session

delete.description: Delete metadata for a specific session

post.description:
Create an event in a session.

This endpoint allows adding events to sessions for synchronization purposes.
Accepts authentication via API key (for agent bots) or bearer token.

Process A2A messages according to official specification.

Supports:
- message/send: Send a message and get response
- message/stream: Send a message and stream response

get.description: Get agent card according to A2A specification.
Health check for A2A official implementation - 100% A2A spec compliant.
List sessions for an agent and external_id (A2A extension).
Get conversation history for a specific session (A2A extension).

post.description:
Get conversation history according to A2A specification.

Endpoint for retrieving multi-turn conversation context.
This implements context preservation as defined in A2A spec.

get.description: List all available tools with optional filtering
Get details of a specific tool
List all tool categories

post.description: Reload tools configuration from file (admin only)

Generate OAuth 2.0 authorization URL for Google Calendar.

Returns an authorization URL that the user should visit to grant access.

Complete OAuth 2.0 authorization flow.

Exchanges the authorization code for access tokens and fetches
available calendars.

get.description:
Get list of available Google Calendars.

Returns all calendars accessible by the authenticated user.

put.description:
Save Google Calendar configuration.

Stores calendar selection and settings for the agent.

delete.description:
Disconnect Google Calendar integration.

Revokes access tokens and deletes stored credentials and configuration.

post.description:
Check calendar availability for a time range.

Returns whether the calendar is available and lists free time slots.

Create a new calendar event.

Creates an event with optional Google Meet link and attendees.

get.description:
OAuth 2.0 callback endpoint for Google Calendar (fixed URL).

This endpoint handles the OAuth redirect from Google Cloud Console.
The account_id and agent_id are extracted from the state parameter.

Query Parameters:
    code: Authorization code from Google OAuth
    state: Base64-encoded JSON containing account_id and agent_id

post.description:
Generate OAuth 2.0 authorization URL for Google Sheets.

Returns an authorization URL that the user should visit to grant access.

Complete OAuth 2.0 authorization flow.

Exchanges the authorization code for access tokens and fetches
available spreadsheets.

get.description:
Get list of available Google Sheets spreadsheets.

Returns all spreadsheets accessible by the authenticated user.

put.description:
Save Google Sheets configuration.

Stores spreadsheet selection and settings for the agent.

delete.description:
Disconnect Google Sheets integration.

Revokes access tokens and deletes stored credentials and configuration.

get.description:
OAuth 2.0 callback endpoint for Google Sheets (fixed URL).

This endpoint handles the OAuth redirect from Google Cloud Console.
The account_id and agent_id are extracted from the state parameter.

Query Parameters:
    code: Authorization code from Google OAuth
    state: Base64-encoded JSON containing account_id and agent_id

post.description:
Discover OAuth requirements from GitHub MCP server.

This endpoint follows the MCP OAuth Protected Resource discovery pattern:
1. Calls the MCP endpoint (expects 401)
2. Extracts OAuth metadata from .well-known/oauth-protected-resource
3. Returns OAuth metadata (authorization_servers, scopes_supported, etc.)

Generate OAuth 2.0 authorization URL for GitHub MCP.

First discovers OAuth requirements from the MCP server, then generates
the authorization URL using the discovered authorization server and scopes.

Returns an authorization URL that the user should visit to grant access.

Complete OAuth authorization flow and store tokens.

This endpoint handles the OAuth callback from GitHub.

get.description: Get GitHub integration configuration.
Discover available MCP tools from GitHub using stored access_token.

OAuth 2.0 callback endpoint for GitHub MCP (fixed URL).

This endpoint handles the OAuth redirect from GitHub.
The account_id and agent_id are extracted from the state parameter.

Query Parameters:
    code: Authorization code from GitHub OAuth
    state: Base64-encoded JSON containing account_id, agent_id, and mcp_url

Get all integration configurations at once, including credentials status.

Returns a dictionary with:
- configs: Dictionary mapping provider names to their configurations
- credentials_configured: Dictionary mapping provider names to boolean status
Comprehensive health check including database, Redis, and system components
Readiness check to verify if the server is ready to serve requests

200.description: System is ready to serve requests

503.description: System is not ready to serve requests

get.description: Kubernetes liveness probe - simple check if app is running
Kubernetes readiness probe - checks if app is ready to serve traffic
